rationale: The "fail bait" hook concept (cursor-driven mistake + provocative "can you win?" text) is a proven hypercasual UA pattern that overlaps with Color Road!'s broad casual audience, but the specific execution is a poor fit: buses, crashes, and a green/red/white palette have no relationship to the game's minimalist pastel rolling-ball-and-color-match DNA. Mechanic match is weak because steering a bus into traffic doesn't translate the color-matching/dodging core loop — viewers who click expecting traffic puzzles will mis-install. Recommendation: borrow the fail-bait STRUCTURE (cursor steers ball into wrong-colored obstacle, "why is this so hard?" overlay) but do not reuse this asset; rebuild it natively in the game's pastel/dark-track aesthetic.
